evidence: stop() (pipeline-bus.ts:212-227) drains Critical then Main via `void this.dispatchSync(pkt)`. dispatchSync (362-368) wraps dispatch in try/catch. BUT dispatch (304-346) already catches EACH handler error in a per-handler try/catch: lines 338-344 `try { await handler(pkt); } catch (err) { this.emitHandlerError(pkt, err); }`. emitHandlerError (348-359) builds a `pipeline.error` PipelineErrorPacket and `this.push(Route.Critical, errorPkt)` (358). The stop loop `while (this.critical.length > 0)` (215) keeps draining, so that error packet is re-dispatched to the registered listener `this.bus.on("pipeline.error", this.handleComponentError...)` (voice-agent-session.ts:496). So handler errors are NOT swallowed by dispatchSync — they are surfaced. dispatchSync's outer catch can only catch errors dispatch ITSELF throws (handler invocation is always wrapped), which does not happen from a handler. Also, the 'plugin partially cleaned' framing is wrong: plugin teardown is runFinalizeChain (init-chain.ts:127, 'Log but continue — finalize must complete'), awaited at voice-agent-session.ts:393 BEFORE bus.stop() at :396. No 'shutdown handlers' are registered on the bus — stop() is a queue flush, not a cleanup-handler dispatcher.
real_fix: No fix needed. Handler errors during stop() are routed through dispatch's per-handler catch -> emitHandlerError -> pipeline.error on Critical, and the stop drain loop re-dispatches that to handleComponentError. Plugin cleanup is a separate awaited mechanism (runFinalizeChain) that runs before bus.stop() and logs/continues on error. (One unrelated and out-of-scope nuance: stop() voids the async dispatchSync promises, so error packets emitted from an *async* handler that awaits would push to Critical on a later microtask after the synchronous while-loops finish — a fire-and-forget timing gap, not the swallowed-error claim kimi made. Not worth flagging as the cited finding.)
